stories.title: Huge crowds in Mashhad as Iran's late supreme leader is buried
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei was buried at the Imam Reza shrine, Iran's holiest Shia Muslim site, ending six days of public mourning ceremonies.

stories.title: Ukrainian agent accused of murdering Monaco bomb suspect changes story
stories.summary: Two men with links to Ukraine's security services are accused of killing Anastasiia Berezovska and dumping her body in woods.

stories.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines

stories.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say

stories.title: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says
stories.summary: It follows similar landings of reusable rockets by US-owned companies SpaceX and Blue Origin.

stories.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn in mega US share sale
stories.summary: The shares are set to start trading on the Nasdaq on Friday in what will be the largest ever debut by a foreign firm.

stories.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
stories.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

stories.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
stories.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

stories.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
stories.summary: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

stories.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high
stories.summary: Investors increasingly view China's two-speed growth with robust export and tepid domestic demand as a defining long-term feature of the Chinese economy.

stories.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
stories.summary: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

stories.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
stories.summary: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

stories.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to rise by 115,000 in June and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

stories.title: Europe wants to rebalance trade with Beijing, but can't quit Chinese air conditioners
stories.summary: A historic heat wave has driven unprecedented demand for Chinese-made air conditioners in Europe, underscoring the tough task Brussels faces in rebalancing trade with Beijing.

stories.title: World Cup could boost the June jobs report by 40,000, Goldman estimates

stories.title: VW Group and unions disagree on plan to streamline the automaker
stories.summary: VW's plan calls for half as many models but didn't mention closures or job cuts.

stories.summary: The Fed named a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen to co-lead an AI productivity and jobs task force under Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy review.

stories.summary: A MiCA license allows crypto firms to operate in the EU, but the ESMA’s review will test whether custodians can meet the required security and resilience standards.
